heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by George Cukor. Written by Philip Barry (play), Donald Ogden Stewart and Waldo Salt.
clean: Directed by George Cukor. Written by Philip Barry (play), Donald Ogden Stewart and Waldo Salt.

heading: Dinah Lord
raw: Oh, I wish something would happen! Nothing ever possibly in the least ever happens here!
clean: Oh, I wish something would happen! Nothing ever possibly in the least ever happens here!

heading: Dinah Lord
raw: [watching the wedding, triumphant] I did it. I did it all!
clean: I did it. I did it all!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: If you just face the facts squarely as I did.
clean: If you just face the facts squarely as I did.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Margaret: We both might face the fact that neither of us have proved to be a very great success as a wife.
clean: We both might face the fact that neither of us have proved to be a very great success as a wife.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: We just picked the wrong first husbands, that's all.
clean: We just picked the wrong first husbands, that's all.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mike: Or: 'What the Kitchen Maid Saw Through the Keyhole.' unquote...quote 'No hunter of buckshot in the rear is Cagey Crafty Connor' unquote, close paragraph.
clean: Or: 'What the Kitchen Maid Saw Through the Keyhole.' unquote...quote 'No hunter of buckshot in the rear is Cagey Crafty Connor' unquote, close paragraph.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Liz: Close job, close bank account. But Mr. Kidd, how can you possibly get inside the Lord estate, let alone the house?
clean: Close job, close bank account. But Mr. Kidd, how can you possibly get inside the Lord estate, let alone the house?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mike: Now we're not gonna do it, Liz, dawgonnit, it's degrading. It's undignified.
clean: Now we're not gonna do it, Liz, dawgonnit, it's degrading. It's undignified.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Liz: And so is an empty stomach. How do we get in?
clean: And so is an empty stomach. How do we get in?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Orange juice, certainly.
clean: Orange juice, certainly.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Don't tell me you've forsaken your beloved whiskey and whiskeys.
clean: Don't tell me you've forsaken your beloved whiskey and whiskeys.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: No, no, no, no. I've just changed their color, that's all. I'm going for the pale pastel shades now. They're more becoming to me. How about you, Mr. Connor? You drink, don't you? Alcohol, I mean.
clean: No, no, no, no. I've just changed their color, that's all. I'm going for the pale pastel shades now. They're more becoming to me. How about you, Mr. Connor? You drink, don't you? Alcohol, I mean.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mike: Oh, a little.
clean: Oh, a little.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: A little, 'little.' And you a writer? I thought all writers drank to excess and beat their wives. You know one time, I think I secretly wanted to be a writer.
clean: A little, 'little.' And you a writer? I thought all writers drank to excess and beat their wives. You know one time, I think I secretly wanted to be a writer.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: I never saw you looking better, Red. You're getting that fine, tawny look.
clean: I never saw you looking better, Red. You're getting that fine, tawny look.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Oh, we're going to talk about me, are we? Goody.
clean: Oh, we're going to talk about me, are we? Goody.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Only as it happens, I'm not interested in myself, for the moment.
clean: Only as it happens, I'm not interested in myself, for the moment.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Not interested in yourself! You're fascinated, Red. You're far and away your favorite person in the world.
clean: Not interested in yourself! You're fascinated, Red. You're far and away your favorite person in the world.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Dexter, in case you don't know it -
clean: Dexter, in case you don't know it -

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Of course, Mr. Connor, she's a girl who's generous to a fault.
clean: Of course, Mr. Connor, she's a girl who's generous to a fault.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: To a fault, Mr. Connor.
clean: To a fault, Mr. Connor.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Except to other people's faults. For instance, she never had any understanding of my deep and gorgeous thirst.
clean: Except to other people's faults. For instance, she never had any understanding of my deep and gorgeous thirst.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: That was your problem.
clean: That was your problem.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Granted. But you took on that problem with me when you took me, Red. You were no help-mate there. You were a scold.
clean: Granted. But you took on that problem with me when you took me, Red. You were no help-mate there. You were a scold.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: It was disgusting. It made you so unattractive.
clean: It was disgusting. It made you so unattractive.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: A weakness, sure, and strength is her religion, Mr. Connor. She finds human imperfection unforgiveable. And when I gradually discovered that my relationship to her was supposed to be not that of a loving husband and a good companion, but - [He turns away from her] Oh, never mind.
clean: A weakness, sure, and strength is her religion, Mr. Connor. She finds human imperfection unforgiveable. And when I gradually discovered that my relationship to her was supposed to be not that of a loving husband and a good companion, but - Oh, never mind.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Say it.
clean: Say it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: But that of a kind of high priest to a virgin goddess, then my drinks grew deeper and more frequent, that's all. [Mike slides off his chair and leaves them.]
clean: But that of a kind of high priest to a virgin goddess, then my drinks grew deeper and more frequent, that's all.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: I never considered you as that, nor myself.
clean: I never considered you as that, nor myself.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: You did without knowing it. Oh, and the night that you got drunk on champagne and climbed out on the roof and stood there, NAKED, with your arms out to the moon, wailing like a banshee - [Dexter laughs at the thought.]
clean: You did without knowing it. Oh, and the night that you got drunk on champagne and climbed out on the roof and stood there, NAKED, with your arms out to the moon, wailing like a banshee -

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: I told you I never had the slightest recollection of doing any such thing.
clean: I told you I never had the slightest recollection of doing any such thing.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: I know. You drew a blank. You wanted to. Mr. Connor, what would you... [He turns and notices Mike has gone] Oh.
clean: I know. You drew a blank. You wanted to. Mr. Connor, what would you... Oh.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: A nice story for spies, incidentally.
clean: A nice story for spies, incidentally.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Too bad we can't supply photographs of you on the roof.
clean: Too bad we can't supply photographs of you on the roof.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: [about marrying George] How in the world could you even think of it?
clean: How in the world could you even think of it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Because he is everything you're not. He's been poor. He's had to work and he's had to fight for everything. And I love him, as I never even began to love you.
clean: Because he is everything you're not. He's been poor. He's had to work and he's had to fight for everything. And I love him, as I never even began to love you.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Maybe so, but I doubt it. I think he's just a swing from me. But it's too violent a swing. Kittredge is no great tower of strength, you know, Tracy. He's just a tower.
clean: Maybe so, but I doubt it. I think he's just a swing from me. But it's too violent a swing. Kittredge is no great tower of strength, you know, Tracy. He's just a tower.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: You hardly know him.
clean: You hardly know him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: To hardly know him is to know him well. And perhaps it offends my vanity to have anyone who is even remotely my wife re-marry so obviously beneath her.
clean: To hardly know him is to know him well. And perhaps it offends my vanity to have anyone who is even remotely my wife re-marry so obviously beneath her.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: How dare you! Any of you in this day and age use such an idiotic...
clean: How dare you! Any of you in this day and age use such an idiotic...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: I'm talking about the difference in mind and spirit...Kittredge is not for you.
clean: I'm talking about the difference in mind and spirit...Kittredge is not for you.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: You bet he's for me. He's a great man and a good man. Already, he's of national importance.
clean: You bet he's for me. He's a great man and a good man. Already, he's of national importance.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: You sound like Spy Magazine talking. But whatever he is, toots, you'll have to stick. He'll give you no out as I did.
clean: You sound like Spy Magazine talking. But whatever he is, toots, you'll have to stick. He'll give you no out as I did.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: I won't require one.
clean: I won't require one.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: I suppose you'd still be attractive to any man of spirit, though. There's something engaging about it, this goddess business. There's something more challenging to the male than the, uh, more obvious charms.
clean: I suppose you'd still be attractive to any man of spirit, though. There's something engaging about it, this goddess business. There's something more challenging to the male than the, uh, more obvious charms.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Really?
clean: Really?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Really. We're very vain, you know - 'This citadel can and shall be taken, and I'm the boy to do it.'
clean: Really. We're very vain, you know - 'This citadel can and shall be taken, and I'm the boy to do it.'

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: You seem quite contemptuous of me all of a sudden.
clean: You seem quite contemptuous of me all of a sudden.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: No, Red, not of you, never of you. Red, you could be the finest woman on this earth. I'm contemptuous of something inside of you you either can't help, or make no attempt to; your so-called 'strength' - your prejudice against weakness - your blank intolerance.
clean: No, Red, not of you, never of you. Red, you could be the finest woman on this earth. I'm contemptuous of something inside of you you either can't help, or make no attempt to; your so-called 'strength' - your prejudice against weakness - your blank intolerance.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Is that all?
clean: Is that all?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: So help me, Dexter, if you say another word, I'll...
clean: So help me, Dexter, if you say another word, I'll...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: I'm through, Red. For the moment, I've had my say.
clean: I'm through, Red. For the moment, I've had my say.

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: You know, we're gonna represent something, Tracy, you and I in our home, something straight, sound, and fine. Then perhaps your friend Mr. Haven will be somewhat less condescending.
clean: You know, we're gonna represent something, Tracy, you and I in our home, something straight, sound, and fine. Then perhaps your friend Mr. Haven will be somewhat less condescending.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: George, you, you don't really mind him, do you? I mean, the fact of him...I mean...that he ever was my lord and master. That we ever were...
clean: George, you, you don't really mind him, do you? I mean, the fact of him...I mean...that he ever was my lord and master. That we ever were...

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: I don't believe he ever was, Tracy, not really. I don't believe that anyone ever was - or ever will be. That's the wonderful thing about you, Tracy.
clean: I don't believe he ever was, Tracy, not really. I don't believe that anyone ever was - or ever will be. That's the wonderful thing about you, Tracy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: What? How?
clean: What? How?

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: Well, you're like some marvelous, distant, well, queen, I guess. You're so cool and fine and - and always so much your own. There's a kind of beautiful purity about you, Tracy, like, like a statue...
clean: Well, you're like some marvelous, distant, well, queen, I guess. You're so cool and fine and - and always so much your own. There's a kind of beautiful purity about you, Tracy, like, like a statue...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: George -
clean: George -

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: Oh, it's grand, Tracy. It's what everybody feels about you. It's what I first worshiped you for from afar.
clean: Oh, it's grand, Tracy. It's what everybody feels about you. It's what I first worshiped you for from afar.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: George, listen -
clean: George, listen -

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: First, now, and always! Only from a little nearer now, eh, darling!
clean: First, now, and always! Only from a little nearer now, eh, darling!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: I-I don't want to be worshiped. I want to be loved!
clean: I-I don't want to be worshiped. I want to be loved!

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: Well, you're that too, Tracy. Oh, you're that all right.
clean: Well, you're that too, Tracy. Oh, you're that all right.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: I mean really loved.
clean: I mean really loved.

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: But that goes without saying, Tracy.
clean: But that goes without saying, Tracy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: No. No, now it's you who doesn't see what I mean.
clean: No. No, now it's you who doesn't see what I mean.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mike: The prettiest sight in this fine, pretty world is the privileged class enjoying its privileges.
clean: The prettiest sight in this fine, pretty world is the privileged class enjoying its privileges.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: You're a snob, Connor.
clean: You're a snob, Connor.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mike: No doubt, no doubt...Tracy. You can't marry that guy.
clean: No doubt, no doubt...Tracy. You can't marry that guy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: George? I'm going to. Why, why not?
clean: George? I'm going to. Why, why not?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mike: Well, I don't know. I thought I'd be for it at first, but you just don't seem to match up.
clean: Well, I don't know. I thought I'd be for it at first, but you just don't seem to match up.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Then the fault's with me.
clean: Then the fault's with me.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mike: Well, maybe so, but all the same now, you can't do it.
clean: Well, maybe so, but all the same now, you can't do it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Come around about noon tomorrow. I mean today. Snob.
clean: Come around about noon tomorrow. I mean today. Snob.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mike: What do ya mean, snob?
clean: What do ya mean, snob?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: You're the worst kind there is. An intellectual snob. You made up your mind awfully young, it seems to me.
clean: You're the worst kind there is. An intellectual snob. You made up your mind awfully young, it seems to me.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mike: Well, thirty's about time to make up your mind. And I'm nothing of the sort, not Mr. Connor.
clean: Well, thirty's about time to make up your mind. And I'm nothing of the sort, not Mr. Connor.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: The time to make up your mind about people - is never.
clean: The time to make up your mind about people - is never.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: [after Dexter has proposed] Dexter, are you sure?
clean: Dexter, are you sure?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Not in the least. But I'll risk it. Will you?
clean: Not in the least. But I'll risk it. Will you?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: You bet! You didn't do it just to soften the blow?
clean: You bet! You didn't do it just to soften the blow?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Nor to save my face?
clean: Nor to save my face?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Oh, it's a nice little face.
clean: Oh, it's a nice little face.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Oh Dexter, I'll be yar now, I promise to be yar.
clean: Oh Dexter, I'll be yar now, I promise to be yar.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dexter: Be whatever you like, you're my redhead.
clean: Be whatever you like, you're my redhead.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: How do I look?
clean: How do I look?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Lord: Like a queen - like a goddess.
clean: Like a queen - like a goddess.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: And do you know how I feel?
clean: And do you know how I feel?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tracy: Like a human. Like a human being.
clean: Like a human. Like a human being.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. Lord: Do you know how I feel?
clean: Do you know how I feel?
